🖥️ When quantum computers become commercially available, what was considered best-in-class encryption will be easily cracked. Even now, threat actors are actively stealing encrypted data from organizations with the intention to break into it with quantum computers in coming years. These are known as “harvest now, decrypt later” attacks.

This is forcing organizations to move towards post-quantum cryptography (PQC)—stronger encryption that can’t be broken by quantum computers. Zscaler has launched new Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) Visibility Reporting and Logging features to help our customers on their transition to PQC: our platform's new interactive reporting, logging and NSS data fields provide deep visibility into traffic using PQC algorithms so customers can:

🔍 Perform crypto inventory discovery
✅ Meet compliance and regulatory requirements
🔐 Secure communications against quantum threat attacks

Learn more → https://bit.ly/3KQQQpu

#QuantumComputing #Crypto #Encryption

🫠 When the Red Sea cable cut disrupted 17% of global internet traffic, businesses worldwide felt the impact.

SaaS apps ran slower, video calls froze, and work was interrupted — causing frustration for users and tech teams alike. This problem with important undersea cables like SMW-4 and IMEWE showed how internet issues can affect businesses and people across many areas and industries.

For IT teams, these challenges demand quick, clear solutions. Blind spots can waste time, cause miscommunication with leadership, and lower productivity.
